programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
1963. Who Killed Holly Howard? Directed by Hy Averback. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. A Model is found murdered, and the only clues seem to link a trio of wealthy Texans to her death.
1965. Viva Diaz!. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
Checkmate. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. Hearing that the Count de Waldern is planning to conscript 3,000 recruits to terrorise the community, Robin hatches a plan.
The Ladies. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Petra Davies & Terence Cooper. A promised shipload of women approaches New Providence. Tempest is concerned Blackbeard may be aware and decides to lay a trap.
The Baroness. 19Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. When her husband is invited to visit the castle of a baroness, Hedda suspects the woman of working for Gessler.
Enjoy an excellent performance from the fabulous Dionne Warwick as she sings the 60s tune Walk On By.
Shoot The Moon. Directed by Aaron Spelling. The U.S Secretary of State requests that Ringo help an Italian astronomer take pictures of a solar eclipse from on top of Superstition Mountain.
1950. Thriller. Directed by Cecil H. Williamson. Stars John Witty, Genine Graham, Patience Rentoul & Gerald Nodin. A doctor is framed for murder.
1935. Drama. Director: Bernard Vorhaus. Bob (Julien Mitchell) is distraught with his upcoming forced retirement and believes his wife is having an affair. The day arrives for his final journey.
1943. War drama starring Richard Arlen, Jean Parker & Russell Hayden. A former naval deserter is drawn back to the services after the attack on Pearl Harbour.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Ganges Might Lead Anywhere
programmes.show_description: 1968. An intriguing look at the many different kinds of religion found in India.
1939. Drama. The Head of Dartmouth training college is murdered but was he the intended victim? Directed by Maurice Elvey. Starring Leslie Banks and Kay Walsh.
1942. Mystery. Directed by Roy William Neill. Stars Basil Rathbone, Nigel Bruce & Lionel Atwill. Sherlock Holmes must protect a scientist's invention from falling into the wrong hands.
1959. Drama. Director: Godfrey Grayson. Stars Sean Lynch, Beth Rogan & Raymond Huntley. A teenager is falsely accused of robbery. A young girl must persuade him to give himself up.
1957. Action. Directed by Jack Lee. Stars Peter Finch, Ronald Lewis and Maureen Swanson. Two brothers join their father in Captain Starlight's (Finch) bushranger gang in 19th century Australia.
1953. Directed by Lewis Gilbert. Starring Jack Warner, Robert Beatty and Anthony Steel. With the help of a life-like dummy, Allied forces are escaping a POW camp. Based on a true story.
The Broken Link. Stars Ron Randell, Peter Neil & Philip Friend. Two business partners receive a letter from a client threatening prosecution. To avoid prison they come up with a plan to murder.
Ahoy Radio Caroline fans, a very rare reel takes us behind the scenes in 1965. Enjoy a beautiful reel of some heritage trains from 1968 and Mike and Noel talk Man From U.N.C.L.E Membership!
1989. Stars Diana Rigg, James Wilby & David McCallum. Kit and Angela plan to wed after a whirlwind romance. All goes well until Kit's reluctance to introduce Angela to his mother Helena.
1970. Horror. Director: Andy Milligan. Stars Gavin Reed, Jackie Skarvellis & Berwick Kaler. A family of vampires are searching for new blood since a inbreeding is destroying the family line...
1962. Drama. Directed by Peter Maxwell. Stars Patrick Holt, Emrys Jones & Honor Blackman. An inspector strives to solve the mystery of an artist's dead wife. The artist becomes the main suspect.
The Blue Carbuncle. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read where Sherlock Holmes investigates a rare gem known as the Blue Carbuncle.
Best Mates. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie comes to the aid of his best friend Maguire, but this simply leads them both into further bother.
1993. Drama. Director: Emir Kusturica. Stars Johnny Depp, Faye Dunaway, Vincent Gallo. A young New Yorker goes to Arizona for a wedding and becomes intertwined in the lives of two eccentric women.
1949. A mysterious baby is found in a boat at sea, along with a charming portrait of a community in Coverack. Where is he from? Stars Dorothea Paul Directed by Anthony Mavrogordato.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life There Ought To Be a Law
programmes.show_description: 1968. With Sam Kydd. A light-hearted look at some of the laws you never even thought existed, and their pitfalls.
The Squatter. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Jeanne Moody & Steven Geray. Jim Bowie gives an arrogant Southern belle a lesson by persuading her to allow an impoverished family to remain on her land.
1959. Crime. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars Gordon Jackson, Sarah Lawson, Warren Mitchell. A shopkeeper is taken hostage when 3 crooks break into his shop to drill through to the bank next door.
1955. Directed by John Gilling and starring Alex Nicol. Two brothers are embroiled in a racket to smuggle a valuable painting. When a murder is committed they must try and clear their names.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life All in a Day's Work
programmes.show_description: 1969. A look at people who travel the world by air as part of their everyday jobs, including businessmen and women who fly around the world as easily as taking a taxi.

programmes.show_name: Four Star Playhouse A Kiss for Mr Lincoln
programmes.show_description: 1955. Stars Joanne Dru & Dick Foran. Henry tries to persuade Murdock to support Lincoln for president, but Murdock is only interested in Henry's wife...
1948. Horror. Directed by Oswald Mitchell and screenplay by John Gilling. Set in Edinburgh in the 1800s and starring Tod Slaughter as William Hart the infamous body broker.
1958. Thriller. Director: Ronald Kinnoch. Stars Marshall Thompson, John Loder & Henry Oscar. A physicist finds himself drawn into an investigation to track down a spy at his research station.
1964. Thriller. Director: Bryan Forbes. Stars Richard Attenborough, Nanette Newman & Kim Stanley. A medium stages a kidnapping which she will then 'solve' by pretending to be psychic.
1954. Comedy. Director: Val Guest. Stars Frankie Howerd, Margaret Rutherford & Petula Clark. An inept bus driver gets lost in fog with a group of passengers, on a coach laden with stolen gold.
1948. Drama. Directed by Arthur Crabtree. Stars Greta Gynt, John McCallum & Raymond Lovell. A racehorse owner becomes involved in a scandal after losing his money and his fiancée.
The Other Side of Swan. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Nigel Lovell. The governor asks Cobb to go to Melbourne and find his estranged brother who is believed to be dead.
The Finny Foot Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum, Kurt Russell & Leo G. Carroll. Solo is trying to track down a chemical agent that hastens the aging process.
Morna. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. After a beautiful blonde is found murdered her story is unravelled in flashbacks about her relationship to her posh school friends.
2003. Comedy/Drama. Director: Geoffrey Sax. Stars Penelope Keith, June Brown & Alan David. A wealthy housewife and her maid go on the run after attacking a house burglar and leaving him for dead.
Caroline Munro is back in The Cellar Club and ready to bring you another evening full of mystery and suspense with the twisted tale Tennis Court, directed by Cyril Frankel.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Tennis Court. 1984. Stars Peter Graves & Hannah Gordan. A couple move into a house they have inherited, but it soon becomes apparent that the old tennis court is far from normal.
